in_flight: DONE + committed + pushed (cairn 17a60c0). THE THIRD STEP OF THE CODE SPINE: the TESTER — the first real DEVICE (base -> base pair -> tester). TesterDevice(BaseDevice) in cairn/tester/device.py: the system's notary and proving mechanism (MAP.md:569). It (1) subclasses BaseDevice so it carries CP1-CP6 and reports the Form v0 #2 surface (intention->state->settings), becoming the FIRST real subject the base's armed composition trap bites on (test_composition read '0 subclasses' until now); (2) run_proof(path) runs a proof as a subprocess and PRODUCES a VALIDATION record — the ratified EIGHT fields (claim, caller, date, method, verdict, evidence, falsifier, horizon; MAP.md:569). Verdict is READ from the subject's exit code (0->green else red), never granted. FRESH not grafted (like device/shim): UU's tester (unseen_university/devices/tester/, ~1226 lines netpolicy/isolation/sandbox/seal) is the network-sandbox pillar Cairn reaches LATER; only MAP's VALIDATION schema crossed, as a record shape (UU's own seal_confirmed/seal_detail verdict shape did NOT cross). PROVEN: green x2 cwds no install; teeth verified by FALSIFICATION not just assertion — a hollow always-green tester trips test_red_proof_validates_red, a 9-field record trips the schema-field-set tooth; dogfoods (its first act validates the real base proofs, both green). Files: cairn/tester/{__init__.py, device.py, intention+why.json}, cairn/tester/proofs/{test_tester.py, fixtures/green_proof.py, fixtures/red_proof.py}. Charter node_class='device' (first runtime component; base/ is the code-seam it stands on).
next_direction: Akien's ratified ordering (2026-07-17): (1) KERNEL-OWNED NETWORK — the tester's OTHER half (MAP.md:888 pairs 'tester + kernel-owned network'). The network the tester owns so a build's reach is guarded by physics, not trust. UU's netpolicy.py (381 lines) + isolation.py (183) are the quarry to assess graft-vs-fresh — expect FRESH-with-schema-carried like the tester, but the isolation mechanism (namespaces/supervisor) may cross more literally since it's OS plumbing, not design Cairn replaced. This ALSO retires two CLAUDE.md 'rules awaiting physics': 'the inference host is reached only through inference_domain' and 'port 5432 only through db_domain' both presuppose the kernel owning the network. (2) THEN db_domain — gives VALIDATIONS a DURABLE home (retires the tester's open edge (a): produced-not-persisted) and retires CLAUDE.md's 'durable state goes through db_domain / store primitives' IOU. Akien's KEY RATIONALE: at network+db we START BUILDING THINGS THAT USE BOTH, and 'the tester will validate everything about db_domain as we go — more dogfood.' So db_domain is built UNDER the tester's proof gate from its first commit; the tester's produced VALIDATIONS get their persistent sink exactly when db_domain lands (the two open edges close each other).
open_threads: CAST + PROVEN this session (verify pointers, Law 3): cairn/tester/ (5 files + __init__). Reconfirm: `python3 cairn/tester/proofs/test_tester.py` (exit 0), plus base still green (`python3 cairn/base/proofs/test_composition.py`, `python3 cairn/base/proofs/test_core_values.py`). All in commit 17a60c0, pushed.

TESTER OPEN EDGES filed not faked (children of this stone, each waits on a real need): (a) VALIDATIONS are PRODUCED, not PERSISTED — durable greppable store is db_domain's stone (CLAUDE.md pending rule: durable state via store primitives); class-space stays state-free. CLOSES when db_domain lands (see next_direction). (b) kernel-owned network + build isolation NOT built — this tester runs LOCAL proofs, does not yet guard a build's network. CLOSES with the network step (the immediate next stone). (c) verdict METHOD is exit-code only — quorum / review-by-N-experts for concept pieces (MAP.md:752) is a later method the `method` field already names room for. (d) reported-vs-filed intention drift detection NOT wired — intention() returns a live dict; comparing it to the charter is a later capability.

RATIFIED THIS SESSION (Akien, 2026-07-17): both base-pair design calls — (1) BaseDevice = Form v0's three ordered reports NOT UU's 14 methods (as cast); (2) BaseShim lifecycle + one-loop deferral, with the governing principle 'shim first, we keep adding things as we go along, and that's ok'. Recorded in cairn/base/intention+why.json (commit 84eb9b7).

MINIMALISM ETHOS SHARPENED (Akien, 2026-07-17, now a memory [[build-minimal-grow-against-need]]): 'everything is minimal... we keep ruthlessly simplifying... build the parts and grow them in the face of real needs than do a thing just to say i could.' AND the round-three nuance: 'this IS the third round. we know which big things we need, now the simplest version, easiest to prove. one stone at a time, placed with all the certainty we can muster, knowing tomorrow we'll do even better.' So minimal != don't-know-the-roadmap; the big things ARE known (earned over 3 rounds); what's minimized is the IMPLEMENTATION of each known stone. This governed the tester scope and governs network+db next.

ENVIRONMENT NOTE (unchanged, measured Law 3): PEP 668 externally-managed env blocks system-wide `pip install -e .` (UU uses .venv). All proofs run bare (python3 + sys.path parents[3] bootstrap), no install. The map's 'pip install -e . green at all times' invariant still wants a cairn venv eventually — decision deferred, non-blocking; the tester now owns proof-running in principle but still runs bare like its siblings today.
